title: Diasperus gunnii
taxonomy: Diasperus gunnii is classified as a species of flowering plant. It belongs to the family Santalaceae. The genus Diasperus is a part of the order Santalales. Diasperus gunnii is native to Australia and Tasmania. Its classification is further categorized within the phylum Magnoliophyta.

The plant's properties and uses are not well-documented, but its roots and leaves have been reported to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. intro: Diasperus gunnii, also known as the Gunni or Australian Gunni, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating a range of ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Diasperus gunnii include limonoids, flavonoids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
